planned_changes.description: Publish the untruncated, full covariance_tuning_report.md replacing the truncated artifact preview. Ensure the Full Decision Table and validation sections are visible in the root of the artifact.

Add an explicit, top-level 'Final Decision Table' section (tabular) mapping each recommendation to the exact control-packet row and value, marking safety-critical entries with a 'safety_critical' tag and linking to the specific validation check(s).

Produce intermediate_calculations.csv listing indexed inputs and derived values: position[i], time[i], velocity_segment[i], acceleration_change[i], jerk_approx[i]. Add this CSV to the artifacts folder and reference it from the report.

Create validation_checks_summary.json containing structured checks (id, description, inputs, expected_range, pass/fail criteria, test_scenarios including +/-5% sensitivity cases) and include sample results from the current packet.
Document rubric item weights and the aggregation formula used to compute final_score (e.g., JSON file rubric_weights.json) to reconcile per-item scores with the reported final_score.
Explicitly mark assumed engineering margins (e.g., 20%) as 'ASSUMED / needs_review' in the Assumptions and Exceptions and reflect the same tag in the Final Decision Table.
Include the reproducibility script (e.g., reproducibility_script.js or reproducibility_runner.sh) used in the trace so reviewers can re-run numeric derivations locally.
Request and capture a domain-expert review/sign-off (documented in the artifact metadata) for the final covariance values and any safety-critical recommendations.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve traceability, machine-checkability, and safety-review readiness by publishing the full report (no truncation), adding a structured Final Decision Table, machine-readable intermediate calculations, a validation checklist, and score-aggregation provenance.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Improve traceability, machine-checkability, and safety-review readiness by publishing the full report (no truncation), adding a structured Final Decision Table, machine-readable intermediate calculations, a validation checklist, and score-aggregation provenance.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline deliverable is high-quality: the covariance_tuning_report.md is present, calculations recompute correctly from the supplied control packet, and missing inputs are flagged. The grader and verifier both passed the artifact-contract checks and produced a strong final score. Two minor issues limit full semantic verification and traceability: (1) the artifact preview is truncated (full artifact access is required for complete review), and (2) the report omits an explicit, clearly labeled Final Decision Table and a separate structured validation_checks_summary.json (and the aggregation/weighting method used to compute the reported final_score is not published). Addressing these will make the deliverable fully machine-auditable and easier for a human domain expert to validate for safety-critical use.
